title: Exponent (IIA scale) calibration
summary: Search for the IIA exponent that best reproduces a set of stated market-share proportions, then return simulated shares at that exponent.
description: Performs a bisection search over the exponents parameter to find the value that minimises the sum of squared errors between simulated shares and a user-supplied stated_proportions map. This is used to calibrate the IIA scale parameter so that the model's base scenario shares match independently observed market shares (e.g., from syndicated data, a stated-importance study, or actual sales data). The output reports simulated shares at the optimal exponent.
usage:
[sim_db]
excel = "model.xlsx"

[["cs::sim_exponent_search"]]
scenarios = ["base"]
exponent_range = [0.1, 5.0, 0.001]

["cs::sim_exponent_search".stated_proportions]
ProductA = 0.40
ProductB = 0.35
ProductC = 0.25

arguments.name: stated_proportions
arguments.description: Target market-share proportions for the calibration. Object mapping product_name to a number in [0, 1]. The exponent search minimises the sum of squared errors between the simulated shares (at the candidate exponent) and these stated proportions. Products not listed are excluded from the SSE objective but are still present in the output.

arguments.name: exponent_range
arguments.description: Search range and tolerance for the bisection: [min, max, tolerance]. The search is over the interval [min, max] and stops when the interval width falls below tolerance. Array of exactly 3 numbers. Defaults to [0.1, 5.0, 0.001].

arguments.name: measures
arguments.description: Pipe-separated output measures for the final results run at the optimal exponent.

arguments.name: scenarios
arguments.description: Names of scenarios to calibrate against. Use ["__all__"] for every scenario in the sim_db.

arguments.name: subgroups
arguments.description: Subgroup column names for the final results run. The bisection itself uses only the first subgroup for speed.

arguments.name: weights
arguments.description: Weight column names for the final results run.

arguments.name: throttles
arguments.description: Throttle-set name(s). Omit to use the sim_db default.

arguments.name: considerations
arguments.description: Consideration-set name(s). Omit to use the sim_db default.

arguments.name: include_nets
arguments.description: When true, append net rows summed by product_net group in the final output.

arguments.name: prob_methods
arguments.description: Probability computation method(s) used during the search and the final run. Methods: "sop" — standard share-of-preference (multinomial logit); "topN" where N ≥ 1 — top-N restricted MNL, only the N highest-utility products receive non-zero probability ("top1" is equivalent to first choice); "<top_spec>:<bot_spec>-<att>" — two-level nested logit grouped by scenario attribute <att>; "<bot_spec>-<att>" shorthand sets top_spec=sop. Pipe-separate to blend (e.g. "sop|top2").

arguments.name: trips
arguments.description: Monte Carlo shopping trips per respondent. Must be >= 1.

arguments.name: return_debug
arguments.description: Include per-respondent debug output in the final run.

arguments.name: scenario_chgs
arguments.description: Attribute overrides applied to all candidate exponent runs. Use this to calibrate against a specific attribute configuration that differs from the scenario baseline.

details:
The bisection runs up to 60 iterations over the interval [min, max]. At each candidate exponent, the simulator runs a full sim_run (using the first subgroup only for speed) and computes SSE over the products listed in stated_proportions. The algorithm evaluates both endpoints and the midpoint, then retains the half of the interval closer to the minimum, narrowing until the interval width falls below tolerance.

stated_proportions does not need to sum to 1.0 — the SSE is computed against raw proportions. However, proportions that sum to approximately 1.0 produce the most interpretable calibration.

Products listed in stated_proportions but absent from the sim_db scenario are ignored in the SSE but contribute no error signal. Products in the sim_db but not in stated_proportions are free to find their natural level at the optimal exponent; they appear in the output CSV at that exponent.

The optimal exponent value is encoded in the scenario column of the output: e.g., "base|exp=1.234567". To apply this exponent in subsequent analyses, copy the value into the exponents parameter of sim_run, sim_sensitivities, etc.
value: Writes sim_exponent_search_0.csv. The scenario column is tagged with the optimal exponent, e.g., "base|exp=1.234567". In protocol mode emits event.run.started, event.artifact.created, event.run.finished.
notes: The bisection uses only the first subgroup during the search for speed; the final output CSV is produced with the full subgroup list.
Bisection converges reliably when the objective is unimodal over the search range. Inspect shares at a few nearby exponent values if the result looks unexpected.
The default range [0.1, 5.0] covers the vast majority of practical calibrations. Tighten to [0.5, 3.0] if you have a prior estimate.
The calibrated exponent can then be used in sim_run / sim_sensitivities / etc. via the exponents parameter.

see_also.description: Run the calibrated simulation with exponents = <found value>.

examples.title: Basic exponent search
examples.body:
[sim_db]
excel = "model.xlsx"

[["cs::sim_exponent_search"]]
scenarios = ["base"]
exponent_range = [0.1, 5.0, 0.001]

["cs::sim_exponent_search".stated_proportions]
ProductA = 0.40
ProductB = 0.35
ProductC = 0.25

examples.title: Narrow range, high precision
examples.body:
[sim_db]
excel = "model.xlsx"

[["cs::sim_exponent_search"]]
scenarios = ["base"]
exponent_range = [0.8, 2.0, 0.0001]

["cs::sim_exponent_search".stated_proportions]
ProductA = 0.45
ProductB = 0.30
ProductC = 0.25

examples.title: Calibrate with attribute override
examples.body:
[sim_db]
excel = "model.xlsx"

[["cs::sim_exponent_search"]]
scenarios = ["base"]
exponent_range = [0.1, 5.0, 0.001]

["cs::sim_exponent_search".stated_proportions]
ProductA = 0.40
ProductB = 0.35
ProductC = 0.25

["cs::sim_exponent_search".scenario_chgs.ProductA]
price = 9.99

fields.name: exponent_range
fields.description: Bisection range and tolerance: [min, max, tol]. Default: [0.1, 5.0, 0.001].

fields.name: measures
fields.description: Pipe-separated measures for the final output. Default: share|revenue.

fields.name: scenarios
fields.description: Scenario name(s) to calibrate. Use ["__all__"] for every scenario.

fields.name: subgroups
fields.description: Subgroup column name(s) for final output.

fields.name: throttles
fields.description: Throttle set name(s). Omit to use sim_db default.

fields.name: considerations
fields.description: Consideration set name(s). Omit to use sim_db default.

fields.name: include_nets
fields.description: Add net rows in final output. Default: false.

fields.name: prob_methods
fields.description: Probability method: sop, topN (N≥1), nested <top>:<bot>-<att>, or pipe-blended. Default: sop.

fields.name: trips
fields.description: Monte Carlo trips per respondent. Default: 1.

fields.name: return_debug
fields.description: Include per-respondent debug output. Default: false.

fields.name: scenario_chgs
fields.description: Attribute overrides applied during the search.
